articles.headline: Government Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Focus on Industrial Decarbonization
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming to accelerate the adoption of green hydrogen across key industrial sectors.
• Phase II focuses on developing green hydrogen hubs, establishing robust supply chains, and providing targeted incentives for hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and refineries.
• The mission seeks to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuels, achieve energy independence, and contribute significantly to global climate goals.
• Enhanced R&D investments and skill development initiatives are crucial components, ensuring India's leadership in the emerging green hydrogen economy.
• This phase builds upon the initial success in establishing pilot projects and aims for large-scale commercial deployment.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy

articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Former Maharashtra Minister Baba Siddique Shot Dead in Mumbai, Security Tightened
articles.explainer:
• Former Maharashtra state minister and veteran Congress leader Baba Siddique was shot dead in Mumbai by unidentified assailants.
• The incident occurred late evening, sending shockwaves through the political fraternity and raising concerns about law and order in the state.
• Mumbai Police have launched a massive manhunt for the perpetrators, with multiple teams formed to investigate the high-profile assassination.
• Political leaders across the spectrum condemned the attack, calling for swift justice and a thorough probe into the motives behind the killing.
• The event has sparked debates on political violence, organized crime, and the security of public figures in metropolitan areas.

Role of civil services in a democracy
Law and order and internal security issues

Evaluate the role of media and civil society in responding to such incidents and demanding accountability.
articles.imageDescription: Police cordon around a crime scene in an urban area.
articles.trivia: Mumbai was originally an archipelago of seven islands, which were gradually merged through land reclamation projects over centuries.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Focuses on Inflation Management Amid Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at its latest bi-monthly review.
• The decision reflects the MPC's cautious approach, balancing inflation control with supporting economic growth amidst global geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to bringing inflation within the target band, while also assessing the impact of previous rate hikes on domestic demand.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted strong domestic economic activity but warned of potential headwinds from global slowdown and supply chain disruptions.
• The central bank's stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy and its instruments
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.

RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing RBI's monetary policy decisions in the current global and domestic economic scenario.
Discuss the impact of maintaining the repo rate on inflation, economic growth, and investment in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in achieving its twin objectives.
Examine the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing inflation control with supporting economic recovery.

articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India-Africa Forum Summit Concludes with Joint Declaration on Enhanced Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing deepened cooperation across various sectors including trade, investment, health, and capacity building.
• Leaders from India and African nations committed to strengthening South-South cooperation, addressing shared challenges like climate change, food security, and regional conflicts.
• India announced new credit lines and technical assistance programs for African partners, focusing on infrastructure development and digital transformation.
• Discussions also covered UN Security Council reforms, with both sides reiterating the need for greater representation of developing countries.
• The summit underscored India's commitment to being a reliable and trusted partner for Africa's growth and development, based on mutual respect and shared priorities.

articles.imageDescription: Group photo of Indian and African leaders at a summit.
articles.trivia: India's first Prime Minister, Jawaharlal Nehru, was a strong proponent of Afro-Asian solidarity and non-alignment.

articles.headline: Government Launches New National Policy for Comprehensive Wetland Conservation and Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) unveiled a new National Wetland Conservation Policy.
• The policy aims to strengthen the regulatory framework, promote integrated management plans, and enhance community participation in wetland protection.
• It emphasizes the ecological services provided by wetlands, such as biodiversity support, flood control, and water purification, and their role in climate change mitigation.
• Key provisions include mapping and demarcation of all wetlands, capacity building for local stakeholders, and strict enforcement against encroachment and pollution.
• The policy is expected to streamline efforts for sustainable utilization and conservation of India's diverse wetland ecosystems.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster Management.

Examine the role of wetlands in climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds.
articles.trivia: Chilika Lake in Odisha is the largest brackish water lagoon in India and one of the first Indian wetlands designated as a Ramsar Site.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Disaster Monitoring
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched an advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-XX) onboard the PSLV-CYY rocket.
• The satellite is equipped with high-resolution imaging capabilities and advanced sensors designed for improved disaster monitoring, agricultural mapping, and urban planning.
• This mission further enhances India's remote sensing capabilities, providing critical data for natural resource management and environmental assessment.
• The launch marks another milestone for ISRO, demonstrating its continued prowess in developing and deploying sophisticated space technology.
• Data from EOS-XX is expected to benefit various government agencies, researchers, and farmers by offering timely and precise geospatial information.

articles.trivia: ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) made India the first Asian nation to reach Martian orbit and the first in the world to do so on its maiden attempt.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Aadhaar's Constitutional Validity with Stronger Data Protection Directives
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, reaffirmed the constitutional validity of the Aadhaar program but issued stringent new directives for data protection and privacy.
• The ruling emphasized the need for robust safeguards to prevent misuse of biometric and demographic data, advocating for a stronger data protection law.
• The Court mandated regular audits of Aadhaar data usage by government and private entities, along with enhanced grievance redressal mechanisms for citizens.
• While acknowledging Aadhaar's role in welfare delivery, the judgment stressed that essential services should not be denied due to authentication failures.
• This verdict aims to strike a balance between state's legitimate interests in welfare delivery and an individual's fundamental right to privacy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
prelimsPoints: Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number.
UIDAI is the statutory authority for Aadhaar.

mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on Aadhaar, particularly concerning the Right to Privacy.

Critically assess the role of Aadhaar in financial inclusion and direct benefit transfers, alongside its privacy concerns.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution by a nine-judge bench of the Supreme Court in 2017.

articles.headline: India Boosts Border Infrastructure and Surveillance Along Northern and Eastern Frontiers
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced significant upgrades to border infrastructure, including roads, bridges, and advanced surveillance systems, along India's northern and eastern borders.
• The initiative aims to enhance troop mobility, logistics capabilities, and overall security posture in strategically sensitive regions.
• New-generation surveillance technologies, including drones and integrated sensor networks, are being deployed to monitor infiltration and cross-border activities.
• This move comes amidst ongoing geopolitical developments and is part of India's long-term strategy to bolster its defense preparedness.
• The upgrades also involve improving living conditions for border guarding forces and integrating local communities into border management efforts.

articles.trivia: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is a road construction executive force in India that develops and maintains road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress on National Education Policy 2020 Implementation, Focus on Foundational Literacy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation.
• The review highlighted significant strides in foundational literacy and numeracy, with several states adopting new curricula and pedagogical approaches.
• Challenges related to teacher training, digital infrastructure, and equitable access in remote areas were also discussed, with plans for accelerated action.
• The NEP aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, promoting holistic development and critical thinking.
• Emphasis was placed on leveraging technology for education delivery and ensuring the successful transition to the new 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.

articles.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's education policy, recommending a common education structure and emphasizing science and vocational education.

articles.headline: India's Q2 GDP Growth Projections Remain Robust Amidst Global Slowdown
articles.explainer:
• Leading financial institutions and government agencies released their Q2 (July-September) GDP growth projections for India, indicating continued robust performance.
• Despite global economic headwinds and slowing demand in major economies, India's domestic consumption and investment remain strong drivers of growth.
• Manufacturing and services sectors showed resilience, though agricultural output faced some regional challenges due to monsoon variations.
• Analysts highlighted government capital expenditure and private sector investment as key enablers, along with stable inflation management by the RBI.
• These projections reinforce India's position as one of the fastest-growing major economies globally, attracting significant foreign investment.

mainsPoints: Analyze the key drivers behind India's robust GDP growth amidst a challenging global economic environment.

articles.imageDescription: Financial charts showing upward trends and growth.
articles.trivia: India's economy is the fifth-largest in the world by nominal GDP and the third-largest by purchasing power parity (PPP).

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for Climate Justice and Equity at Pre-COP29 Consultations
articles.explainer:
• India participated in the pre-COP29 consultations, reiterating its long-standing demand for climate justice, equity, and common but differentiated responsibilities (CBDR-RC).
• The Indian delegation emphasized the need for developed nations to meet their commitments on climate finance and technology transfer to developing countries.
• Discussions focused on setting ambitious but equitable targets for emissions reduction and adaptation, ahead of the 29th Conference of Parties (COP29).
• India highlighted its progress in renewable energy deployment and reforestation efforts, urging global collaboration for a just energy transition.
• The consultations aimed to build consensus on key negotiating points for COP29, particularly regarding global stocktake outcomes and new climate finance goals.

articles.imageDescription: Delegates at an international climate conference.
articles.trivia: The Paris Agreement aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared to pre-industrial levels.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Electoral Reforms, Including Digital Voting Trials
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee submitted its report on electoral reforms, proposing a range of measures to enhance transparency and fairness in elections.
• Key recommendations include exploring the feasibility of digital voting trials, strengthening the Election Commission's autonomy, and regulating political party funding.
• The panel also suggested measures to curb the use of money power, address criminalization of politics, and ensure faster disposal of election petitions.
• The report advocates for a clearer legal framework for social media regulation during election campaigns to prevent misinformation.
• These recommendations are aimed at modernizing the electoral process and reinforcing public trust in democratic institutions.

articles.trivia: India uses Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) since 1982, with Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail (VVPAT) introduced nationwide in 2019.

articles.headline: National Clean Air Programme Shows Mixed Progress, Urges Faster Implementation
articles.explainer:
• A review of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) indicated mixed progress across different cities, with some showing significant reduction in particulate matter.
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change highlighted the need for accelerated implementation of city-specific action plans and greater inter-agency coordination.
• Challenges persist in source apportionment studies, robust monitoring networks, and effective enforcement of pollution control norms.
• NCAP aims to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024, relative to 2017 levels, in 131 non-attainment cities.
• The review emphasized the importance of public awareness campaigns and adopting cleaner technologies across industries and transport sectors.

articles.headline: Indian Researchers Announce Breakthroughs in Indigenous Vaccine Development for Tropical Diseases
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientific institutions announced significant breakthroughs in the development of indigenous vaccines for several neglected tropical diseases.
• Researchers have successfully completed advanced clinical trials for a new vaccine candidate against a prevalent vector-borne disease, showing high efficacy.
• This achievement underscores India's growing capabilities in biotechnology and pharmaceutical research, contributing to global health security.
• The development aligns with the 'Make in India' initiative and aims to provide affordable and accessible healthcare solutions for developing countries.
• Government funding and public-private partnerships have been crucial in accelerating the research and development pipeline for these critical vaccines.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Scheme to Promote Women Entrepreneurship and Financial Independence
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched a new flagship scheme aimed at fostering women entrepreneurship and enhancing their financial independence.
• The scheme provides access to easier credit, skill development training, mentorship programs, and market linkages for women-led enterprises.
• It specifically targets women in rural and semi-urban areas, encouraging them to start and scale up their businesses across various sectors.
• The initiative aligns with the government's broader agenda of gender equality and inclusive economic growth.
• Awareness campaigns and digital platforms will be utilized to maximize outreach and ensure seamless access to scheme benefits.

articles.headline: Archaeologists Unearth New Harappan Settlement, Shedding Light on Indus Valley Civilization
articles.explainer:
• Indian archaeologists announced the discovery of a previously unknown Harappan settlement in the Kutch region of Gujarat.
• The site reveals evidence of sophisticated urban planning, advanced water management systems, and unique pottery styles, suggesting a distinct regional variant of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• Initial findings indicate trade links with other major Harappan sites and potentially with contemporary civilizations in West Asia.
• The discovery is expected to provide valuable insights into the social structure, economic activities, and eventual decline of the Harappan civilization.
• Further excavations and carbon dating will be crucial for establishing the chronology and cultural significance of the new site.

articles.trivia: Dholavira, a Harappan city in Gujarat, is unique for its sophisticated water management system and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

articles.headline: Government Enhances Credit Guarantee Scheme for MSMEs to Boost Lending and Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) announced significant enhancements to the Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) scheme.
• The modifications aim to increase the quantum of guaranteed loans, reduce collateral requirements, and simplify the application process for MSMEs.
• This move is expected to boost institutional credit flow to the MSME sector, which is vital for employment generation and economic growth.
• The government emphasized the role of MSMEs in achieving the vision of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) and driving exports.
• Banks and financial institutions are encouraged to leverage the revamped scheme to support a wider range of small businesses, including startups and women-led enterprises.

articles.headline: India Inaugurates Mega Solar Power Park, Boosting Renewable Energy Capacity
articles.explainer:
• A new mega solar power park, one of the largest in the country, was inaugurated in a Western Indian state, significantly augmenting India's renewable energy capacity.
• The park features advanced solar photovoltaic technology and is expected to generate enough clean electricity to power millions of homes.
• This project is a crucial step towards achieving India's ambitious renewable energy targets and reducing its carbon footprint.
• Private sector investment and state-of-the-art engineering were instrumental in the rapid development and commissioning of the solar park.
• The inauguration underscores India's commitment to energy transition and its leadership in the global solar energy landscape.

articles.headline: Indian Navy Reviews Maritime Security Strategy Amidst Evolving Geopolitical Landscape
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy conducted a high-level review of its maritime security strategy, focusing on evolving geopolitical challenges in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• Discussions centered on enhancing naval capabilities, strengthening regional partnerships, and countering emerging threats like piracy, maritime terrorism, and illegal fishing.
• The review emphasized the need for advanced surveillance systems, underwater domain awareness, and seamless interoperability with friendly navies.
• India's strategic interests in maintaining a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific were reiterated, aligning with its SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) vision.
• The outcome of the review will guide future naval acquisitions, training, and deployment strategies to safeguard India's extensive coastline and maritime trade routes.

articles.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches Scheme to Promote Cultural and Heritage Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism introduced a new scheme aimed at boosting cultural and heritage tourism across India, focusing on lesser-known historical sites and traditions.
• The initiative includes infrastructure development, digital promotion, skill training for local guides, and incentives for private sector investment in heritage conservation.
• It seeks to diversify tourism offerings beyond popular destinations, spread economic benefits to local communities, and preserve India's rich cultural heritage.
• Emphasis is placed on creating immersive cultural experiences, promoting local arts and crafts, and integrating sustainable tourism practices.

articles.headline: India Forges Global Partnerships for Digital Public Infrastructure Export
articles.explainer:
• India is actively pursuing global partnerships to export its successful Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) models, such as Aadhaar and UPI, to other developing nations.
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced collaborations with several countries to help them build their own digital identity and payment systems.
• This initiative aims to share India's expertise in digital governance, promote financial inclusion globally, and establish India as a leader in digital transformation.
• The focus is on providing open-source, scalable, and interoperable digital solutions that are adaptable to diverse national contexts.
• These partnerships are expected to strengthen India's diplomatic ties and foster a digital commons approach to global development.

articles.headline: Animal Welfare Board Issues New Guidelines for Protection of Livestock and Companion Animals
articles.explainer:
• The Animal Welfare Board of India (AWBI) released a comprehensive set of new guidelines aimed at enhancing the protection and welfare of livestock and companion animals.
• The guidelines cover aspects such as humane treatment in transportation, ethical breeding practices, prevention of cruelty, and responsible pet ownership.
• They emphasize stricter enforcement of existing animal protection laws and advocate for increased awareness among the public and stakeholders.
• The move comes amidst rising concerns over animal cruelty and illegal animal trade, prompting calls for more robust legal frameworks.
• AWBI also urged state governments to establish dedicated animal welfare committees and provide adequate resources for animal rescue and rehabilitation.

articles.headline: National Nutrition Mission (POSHAN Abhiyaan) Shows Progress in Reducing Malnutrition
articles.explainer:
• A recent report on the National Nutrition Mission (POSHAN Abhiyaan) highlighted significant progress in reducing stunting, wasting, and anemia among children and women.
• The mission's multi-sectoral approach, integrating health, anganwadi services, and public awareness campaigns, has yielded positive results.
• Challenges remain in achieving universal coverage, particularly in remote and tribal areas, and addressing micronutrient deficiencies.
• POSHAN Abhiyaan aims to improve nutritional outcomes for children, adolescent girls, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.
• The government plans to strengthen community-based interventions and leverage technology for better monitoring and delivery of services.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to achieve an annual green hydrogen production capacity of 5 Million Metric Tonnes (MMT) by 2030.
2. The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) programme is a component of this mission.
3. The Ministry of Power is the nodal ministry for its implementation.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The mission targets 5 MMT annual green hydrogen production capacity by 2030. Statement 2 is correct. SIGHT programme is a key component providing financial incentives. Statement 3 is incorrect. The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) is the nodal ministry for the National Green Hydrogen Mission.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for conducting bi-monthly monetary policy reviews in India?
options: (a) Reserve Bank of India Board of Directors
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body (3 from RBI, 3 appointed by GoI) responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target while keeping in mind the objective of growth.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS):
1. It is a biennial event bringing together leaders from India and African nations.
2. India extends Lines of Credit (LoC) to African countries for development projects under this framework.
3. Both India and African nations advocate for reforms in the United Nations Security Council.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. IAFS is a key platform for India-Africa engagement, involving regular summits, extending Lines of Credit for development, and advocating for reforms in global governance bodies like the UNSC.

prelimsMocks.question: The '5+3+3+4' curricular structure, recently in news, is associated with which of the following government policies?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 proposes a new 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, replacing the old 10+2 system. This structure aims for holistic development from foundational stages through secondary education.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP), consider the following statements:
1. It was launched by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC).
2. Its primary objective is to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024, taking 2017 as the base year.
3. It targets all statutory towns in India for air quality improvement.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; NCAP was launched by MoEFCC. Statement 2 is correct, this is the stated target. Statement 3 is incorrect; NCAP primarily targets 131 'non-attainment cities' that consistently exceed national ambient air quality standards, not all statutory towns.

GS1.headline: Archaeologists Unearth New Harappan Settlement, Shedding Light on Indus Valley Civilization
GS1.explainer:
• Indian archaeologists announced the discovery of a previously unknown Harappan settlement in the Kutch region of Gujarat.
• The site reveals evidence of sophisticated urban planning, advanced water management systems, and unique pottery styles, suggesting a distinct regional variant of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• Initial findings indicate trade links with other major Harappan sites and potentially with contemporary civilizations in West Asia.
• The discovery is expected to provide valuable insights into the social structure, economic activities, and eventual decline of the Harappan civilization.
• Further excavations and carbon dating will be crucial for establishing the chronology and cultural significance of the new site.

History of the World will include events from 18th century such as industrial revolution, world wars, redrawal of national boundaries, colonization, decolonization, political philosophies like communism, capitalism, socialism etc.— their forms and effect on the society.

Examine the significance of water management systems and trade networks in the prosperity of Harappan cities.
Critically assess the theories regarding the decline of the Indus Valley Civilization in light of new evidence.

GS2.headline: Former Maharashtra Minister Baba Siddique Shot Dead in Mumbai, Security Tightened
GS2.explainer:
• Former Maharashtra state minister and veteran Congress leader Baba Siddique was shot dead in Mumbai by unidentified assailants.
• The incident occurred late evening, sending shockwaves through the political fraternity and raising concerns about law and order in the state.
• Mumbai Police have launched a massive manhunt for the perpetrators, with multiple teams formed to investigate the high-profile assassination.
• Political leaders across the spectrum condemned the attack, calling for swift justice and a thorough probe into the motives behind the killing.
• The event has sparked debates on political violence, organized crime, and the security of public figures in metropolitan areas.

GS2.headline: India-Africa Forum Summit Concludes with Joint Declaration on Enhanced Cooperation

• Leaders from India and African nations committed to strengthening South-South cooperation, addressing shared challenges like climate change, food security, and regional conflicts.
• India announced new credit lines and technical assistance programs for African partners, focusing on infrastructure development and digital transformation.
• Discussions also covered UN Security Council reforms, with both sides reiterating the need for greater representation of developing countries.
• The summit underscored India's commitment to being a reliable and trusted partner for Africa's growth and development, based on mutual respect and shared priorities.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Aadhaar's Constitutional Validity with Stronger Data Protection Directives
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, reaffirmed the constitutional validity of the Aadhaar program but issued stringent new directives for data protection and privacy.
• The ruling emphasized the need for robust safeguards to prevent misuse of biometric and demographic data, advocating for a stronger data protection law.
• The Court mandated regular audits of Aadhaar data usage by government and private entities, along with enhanced grievance redressal mechanisms for citizens.
• While acknowledging Aadhaar's role in welfare delivery, the judgment stressed that essential services should not be denied due to authentication failures.
• This verdict aims to strike a balance between state's legitimate interests in welfare delivery and an individual's fundamental right to privacy.

• The focus is on providing open-source, scalable, and interoperable digital solutions that are adaptable to diverse national contexts.
• These partnerships are expected to strengthen India's diplomatic ties and foster a digital commons approach to global development.

GS2.headline: National Nutrition Mission (POSHAN Abhiyaan) Shows Progress in Reducing Malnutrition
GS2.explainer:
• A recent report on the National Nutrition Mission (POSHAN Abhiyaan) highlighted significant progress in reducing stunting, wasting, and anemia among children and women.
• The mission's multi-sectoral approach, integrating health, anganwadi services, and public awareness campaigns, has yielded positive results.
• Challenges remain in achieving universal coverage, particularly in remote and tribal areas, and addressing micronutrient deficiencies.
• POSHAN Abhiyaan aims to improve nutritional outcomes for children, adolescent girls, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.
• The government plans to strengthen community-based interventions and leverage technology for better monitoring and delivery of services.

GS2.trivia: The first National Nutrition Policy was adopted by the Government of India in 1993.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Focus on Industrial Decarbonization
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming to accelerate the adoption of green hydrogen across key industrial sectors.
• Phase II focuses on developing green hydrogen hubs, establishing robust supply chains, and providing targeted incentives for hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and refineries.
• The mission seeks to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuels, achieve energy independence, and contribute significantly to global climate goals.
• Enhanced R&D investments and skill development initiatives are crucial components, ensuring India's leadership in the emerging green hydrogen economy.
• This phase builds upon the initial success in establishing pilot projects and aims for large-scale commercial deployment.

Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life

GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Focuses on Inflation Management Amid Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at its latest bi-monthly review.
• The decision reflects the MPC's cautious approach, balancing inflation control with supporting economic growth amidst global geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to bringing inflation within the target band, while also assessing the impact of previous rate hikes on domestic demand.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted strong domestic economic activity but warned of potential headwinds from global slowdown and supply chain disruptions.
• The central bank's stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.

Inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.

Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in achieving its twin objectives.
Examine the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing inflation control with supporting economic recovery.
Critically assess the role of global factors, such as commodity prices and geopolitical events, on India's monetary policy.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: Government Launches New National Policy for Comprehensive Wetland Conservation and Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) unveiled a new National Wetland Conservation Policy.
• The policy aims to strengthen the regulatory framework, promote integrated management plans, and enhance community participation in wetland protection.
• It emphasizes the ecological services provided by wetlands, such as biodiversity support, flood control, and water purification, and their role in climate change mitigation.
• Key provisions include mapping and demarcation of all wetlands, capacity building for local stakeholders, and strict enforcement against encroachment and pollution.
• The policy is expected to streamline efforts for sustainable utilization and conservation of India's diverse wetland ecosystems.

Examine the role of wetlands in climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds.
GS3.trivia: Chilika Lake in Odisha is the largest brackish water lagoon in India and one of the first Indian wetlands designated as a Ramsar Site.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Disaster Monitoring
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched an advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-XX) onboard the PSLV-CYY rocket.
• The satellite is equipped with high-resolution imaging capabilities and advanced sensors designed for improved disaster monitoring, agricultural mapping, and urban planning.
• This mission further enhances India's remote sensing capabilities, providing critical data for natural resource management and environmental assessment.
• The launch marks another milestone for ISRO, demonstrating its continued prowess in developing and deploying sophisticated space technology.
• Data from EOS-XX is expected to benefit various government agencies, researchers, and farmers by offering timely and precise geospatial information.

GS3.trivia: ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) made India the first Asian nation to reach Martian orbit and the first in the world to do so on its maiden attempt.

GS3.headline: India Boosts Border Infrastructure and Surveillance Along Northern and Eastern Frontiers
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced significant upgrades to border infrastructure, including roads, bridges, and advanced surveillance systems, along India's northern and eastern borders.
• The initiative aims to enhance troop mobility, logistics capabilities, and overall security posture in strategically sensitive regions.
• New-generation surveillance technologies, including drones and integrated sensor networks, are being deployed to monitor infiltration and cross-border activities.
• This move comes amidst ongoing geopolitical developments and is part of India's long-term strategy to bolster its defense preparedness.
• The upgrades also involve improving living conditions for border guarding forces and integrating local communities into border management efforts.

GS3.trivia: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is a road construction executive force in India that develops and maintains road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.

GS3.headline: India's Q2 GDP Growth Projections Remain Robust Amidst Global Slowdown
GS3.explainer:
• Leading financial institutions and government agencies released their Q2 (July-September) GDP growth projections for India, indicating continued robust performance.
• Despite global economic headwinds and slowing demand in major economies, India's domestic consumption and investment remain strong drivers of growth.
• Manufacturing and services sectors showed resilience, though agricultural output faced some regional challenges due to monsoon variations.
• Analysts highlighted government capital expenditure and private sector investment as key enablers, along with stable inflation management by the RBI.
• These projections reinforce India's position as one of the fastest-growing major economies globally, attracting significant foreign investment.

GS3.imageDescription: Financial charts showing upward trends and growth.
GS3.trivia: India's economy is the fifth-largest in the world by nominal GDP and the third-largest by purchasing power parity (PPP).

GS3.headline: India Reiterates Call for Climate Justice and Equity at Pre-COP29 Consultations
GS3.explainer:
• India participated in the pre-COP29 consultations, reiterating its long-standing demand for climate justice, equity, and common but differentiated responsibilities (CBDR-RC).
• The Indian delegation emphasized the need for developed nations to meet their commitments on climate finance and technology transfer to developing countries.
• Discussions focused on setting ambitious but equitable targets for emissions reduction and adaptation, ahead of the 29th Conference of Parties (COP29).
• India highlighted its progress in renewable energy deployment and reforestation efforts, urging global collaboration for a just energy transition.
• The consultations aimed to build consensus on key negotiating points for COP29, particularly regarding global stocktake outcomes and new climate finance goals.

GS3.trivia: The Paris Agreement aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared to pre-industrial levels.

GS3.headline: National Clean Air Programme Shows Mixed Progress, Urges Faster Implementation
GS3.explainer:
• A review of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) indicated mixed progress across different cities, with some showing significant reduction in particulate matter.
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change highlighted the need for accelerated implementation of city-specific action plans and greater inter-agency coordination.
• Challenges persist in source apportionment studies, robust monitoring networks, and effective enforcement of pollution control norms.
• NCAP aims to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024, relative to 2017 levels, in 131 non-attainment cities.
• The review emphasized the importance of public awareness campaigns and adopting cleaner technologies across industries and transport sectors.

GS3.imageDescription: Smoggy urban skyline indicating air pollution.
GS3.trivia: According to the World Health Organization, air pollution is responsible for an estimated 7 million premature deaths worldwide each year.

GS3.headline: Indian Researchers Announce Breakthroughs in Indigenous Vaccine Development for Tropical Diseases
GS3.explainer:
• Indian scientific institutions announced significant breakthroughs in the development of indigenous vaccines for several neglected tropical diseases.
• Researchers have successfully completed advanced clinical trials for a new vaccine candidate against a prevalent vector-borne disease, showing high efficacy.
• This achievement underscores India's growing capabilities in biotechnology and pharmaceutical research, contributing to global health security.
• The development aligns with the 'Make in India' initiative and aims to provide affordable and accessible healthcare solutions for developing countries.
• Government funding and public-private partnerships have been crucial in accelerating the research and development pipeline for these critical vaccines.

GS3.headline: Government Enhances Credit Guarantee Scheme for MSMEs to Boost Lending and Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) announced significant enhancements to the Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) scheme.
• The modifications aim to increase the quantum of guaranteed loans, reduce collateral requirements, and simplify the application process for MSMEs.
• This move is expected to boost institutional credit flow to the MSME sector, which is vital for employment generation and economic growth.
• The government emphasized the role of MSMEs in achieving the vision of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) and driving exports.
• Banks and financial institutions are encouraged to leverage the revamped scheme to support a wider range of small businesses, including startups and women-led enterprises.

GS3.headline: India Inaugurates Mega Solar Power Park, Boosting Renewable Energy Capacity
GS3.explainer:
• A new mega solar power park, one of the largest in the country, was inaugurated in a Western Indian state, significantly augmenting India's renewable energy capacity.
• The park features advanced solar photovoltaic technology and is expected to generate enough clean electricity to power millions of homes.
• This project is a crucial step towards achieving India's ambitious renewable energy targets and reducing its carbon footprint.
• Private sector investment and state-of-the-art engineering were instrumental in the rapid development and commissioning of the solar park.
• The inauguration underscores India's commitment to energy transition and its leadership in the global solar energy landscape.

GS3.headline: Indian Navy Reviews Maritime Security Strategy Amidst Evolving Geopolitical Landscape
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Navy conducted a high-level review of its maritime security strategy, focusing on evolving geopolitical challenges in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• Discussions centered on enhancing naval capabilities, strengthening regional partnerships, and countering emerging threats like piracy, maritime terrorism, and illegal fishing.
• The review emphasized the need for advanced surveillance systems, underwater domain awareness, and seamless interoperability with friendly navies.
• India's strategic interests in maintaining a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific were reiterated, aligning with its SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) vision.
• The outcome of the review will guide future naval acquisitions, training, and deployment strategies to safeguard India's extensive coastline and maritime trade routes.

GS3.headline: Animal Welfare Board Issues New Guidelines for Protection of Livestock and Companion Animals
GS3.explainer:
• The Animal Welfare Board of India (AWBI) released a comprehensive set of new guidelines aimed at enhancing the protection and welfare of livestock and companion animals.
• The guidelines cover aspects such as humane treatment in transportation, ethical breeding practices, prevention of cruelty, and responsible pet ownership.
• They emphasize stricter enforcement of existing animal protection laws and advocate for increased awareness among the public and stakeholders.
• The move comes amidst rising concerns over animal cruelty and illegal animal trade, prompting calls for more robust legal frameworks.
• AWBI also urged state governments to establish dedicated animal welfare committees and provide adequate resources for animal rescue and rehabilitation.
